claim: Senior engineers in 2026 design systems and let AI handle implementation; developers who master this methodology will divide the industry from those who cannot.
actionable: Position yourself as an architect first—design systems, define specs, and use AI for implementation rather than attempting to code manually.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Spec-driven development (specs before code) prevents AI from drifting halfway through a project; vibe coding (describe outcome and let agent run) collapses on week three when new features break old ones.
actionable: Before any AI coding session, write detailed specs defining goals, design decisions, implementation details, and a verification checklist; treat the spec as the contract.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: The six-file context system (project overview, architecture, code standards, AI workflow rules, UI context, progress tracker) is essential for keeping AI agents consistent across a project's entire lifecycle.
actionable: Create and maintain six context files before and during any AI-assisted build: define project goals, tech stack boundaries, code conventions, AI discipline rules, UI tokens, and current phase/decisions.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: A developer who writes 'Build me a SaaS with auth and canvas' vs. one who writes 'I'm adding a LiveBlocks provider to the workspace route with Clerk middleware and React Flow; verify project membership before issuing tokens' will get drastically different AI output; the second is architectural thinking.
actionable: Always provide AI agents with context about your architecture, existing auth layers, component boundaries, and what should NOT be touched; communicate decisions, not wishes.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Agent skills (official skill packages from libraries like Clerk, Prisma, Liveblocks) give AI up-to-date knowledge of APIs, patterns, and best practices; they solve the problem of agents trained on outdated documentation.
actionable: Search for and install official agent skills for every major library you use (e.g., `npx skills add clerk skills`, `npx skills add prisma skills`); tell your agent to consult them.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Next.js 15+ requires `proxy.ts` instead of `middleware.ts` for Clerk auth; agents trained on Next.js 14/15 code will default to the wrong filename unless you explicitly specify it in the spec.
actionable: When using Clerk with Next.js 15+, explicitly specify in your spec that the file must be named `proxy.ts` at the project root, not `middleware.ts`.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Clerk by default leaves all routes public; you must explicitly define which routes are protected and which are public in middleware to enforce auth correctly.
actionable: When integrating Clerk, explicitly configure public routes (sign-in, sign-up, landing) and protect all others by default; do not rely on Clerk to auto-protect routes.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Using a hybrid storage model (Postgres for metadata, Vercel Blob/cloud storage for large artifacts) keeps the database lean and avoids performance issues from storing large JSON or markdown blobs in Postgres.
actionable: For large files (markdown specs, JSON architectures, canvas state), store them in blob/cloud storage and keep only metadata and references in Postgres.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Long-running AI tasks (>60 seconds) timeout in production if called from Next.js API routes; use Trigger.dev for background jobs with retry logic and status tracking.
actionable: For any AI generation or long-running task, route it through Trigger.dev background jobs, not API request handlers, to avoid production timeouts.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Liveblocks rooms are open by default; secure them with authentication endpoints that verify user membership before issuing tokens, ensuring only authorized users can access shared projects.
actionable: For every Liveblocks room, create an auth endpoint (e.g., POST /api/liveblocks/auth) that verifies user membership in the project before issuing room tokens.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Code review tools like Code Rabbit should be integrated into PR reviews to catch AI-generated code issues (security, accessibility, error handling, edge cases) early before merging.
actionable: Add Code Rabbit (or similar AI code review tool) to your PR workflow; review every AI-generated feature with a code review tool before merging to main.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: AI-generated code produces 1.7x more problems per unit code than manually written code; code review is not optional when using AI agents for implementation.
actionable: Plan code review time into your AI-assisted development workflow; do not skip review to save time—review prevents 1.7x more issues.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: When dragging components from a panel into a React Flow canvas, pointer handlers inside React Flow interfere with drag gesture recognition; move drag sources outside React Flow and put on drop handlers on the wrapper, not the Flow component.
actionable: For React Flow drag-and-drop to work, place draggable source elements outside the React Flow component and attach on drop handlers to the outer wrapper div, not the Flow component.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Liveblocks flow storage schema is nested under a 'flow' key, not at the top level; misconfiguring storage type or mutations will cause drag-and-drop and node creation to fail silently.
actionable: When using Liveblocks with React Flow, ensure storage is nested under a 'flow' key and use Liveblocks' `onNodesChange` with type 'add' for node mutations, not direct storage.set calls.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Developers who learn deeply (architecture, systems thinking) and use AI for speed will thrive; those who learned just enough to prompt without understanding the underlying system will get squeezed out as the market automates surface-level work.
actionable: Invest time in learning architectural thinking, systems design, and domain fundamentals; use AI to build faster, not to skip learning.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: Breaking a project into small spec-driven units (one per session, with clear done criteria) prevents AI drift and makes it possible to swap agents or onboard new developers without re-explaining the entire project.
actionable: Create a spec file for each feature unit with a single goal, design decisions, implementation steps, and verification checklist; limit each unit to one focused session.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App

claim: The progress tracker file is the solution to AI's lack of memory between sessions; it records the current phase, completed features, architectural decisions, and session notes so one prompt can restore full context.
actionable: Maintain a progress tracker file throughout the build documenting phase, completed features, decisions, and notes; read it at the start of each new session to restore context.
source_title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App
